vocab.meaning: chi một khoản tiền lớn (thường là một cách khiên cưỡng)
vocab.example: I occasionally shell out for premium leather boots because they tend to last much longer than synthetic ones.
I usually put by a small amount each month so I can afford a high-quality pair of trainers once a year.

vocab.meaning: chấp nhận thiếu thốn, không dùng thứ gì đó (để tiết kiệm)
vocab.example: I’d rather go without luxury items for a few months just to invest in a durable pair of running shoes.

vocab.meaning: mua nhanh chóng (khi có giá hời hoặc hàng hiếm)
vocab.example: Whenever I see a clearance sale, I try to snap up designer heels at a fraction of their original cost.

vocab.meaning: giúp ai đó xoay xở trong lúc khó khăn (về tiền bạc)
vocab.example: I bought a cheap pair of flip-flops to tide me over until I can find the perfect formal shoes for the wedding.
I had to dip into my savings to buy those limited-edition sneakers since they were quite an impulse purchase.

vocab.meaning: khảo giá, xem xét nhiều cửa hàng trước khi mua
vocab.example: I’m not a big spender, so I usually look around different outlets to find the best deal possible.
The shopkeeper was kind enough to knock off ten percent because the shoes had a tiny scuff on the heel.

vocab.meaning: làm tăng nhanh (số nợ, hóa đơn)
vocab.example: It's easy to run up a massive credit card bill if you have a weakness for high-end fashion brands.

vocab.meaning: chi một khoản tiền lớn cho một mục đích cụ thể
vocab.example: Most professional athletes don't mind laying out hundreds of dollars for shoes that offer superior ankle support.
